item.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

item.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

item.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.

item.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

item.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

item.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

item.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

item.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

item.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

item.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk. The goal is less of a […]

item.title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.

item.title: OpenAI’s Jalapeño chip is Big Tech’s spiciest move away from Nvidia
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Anthony Ha, Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk. The goal is less of a […]

item.title: Early Bird pricing ends tonight for TechCrunch Founder Summit
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026. Early Bird pricing ends today, at 11:59 p.m. PT, after which rates increase. Register now.

item.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

item.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Despite ChatGPT's commanding market lead, consumers who pay for AI have been increasingly choosing Anthropic's Claude, data shows.

item.title: General Intuition’s $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: General Intuition has raised $320 million to scale AI trained on millions of hours of gameplay, betting action data can help AI develop something closer to human intuition.

item.title: Databricks’ former AI chief thinks he can cut AI’s power bill by 1,000x
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Un-0 is an image-generation system tool that shows for the first time how the company's technology can replicate conventional AI systems.

item.title: Netris raises $15M Series A from a16z to help AI neoclouds go live faster
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Netris provides software that runs on network switches, and offers a platform that helps neocloud operators reduce the time it takes to go live.

item.title: 2 days left to save up to $190: Join 1,000+ founders and investors at TechCrunch Founder Summit
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Two days left to lock in your spot at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 and save up to $190 before Early Bird rates expire on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register today.
